comment: Soooooo many advantages! Take his class! Don't hesitate!

Professor Maas is very nice and accommodating towards students! You get six late days for the quarter, and he's quick to reply to Ed posts. Also, we can work with partners on group assignments, which allowed for collaboration and meeting new people.
Best prof in the whole Allen school. Wish there were more DB classes to take after 444!

Ryan Maas is easily one of the best professors at the University of Washington. Very clear lectures and super patient with student questions. Made time outside of class in-person, in-office hours or EdStem, and right after class. Gave a reasonable amount of homework which covers class content well. Also understanding with personal matters! 10/10 :)
Very nice professor and a great lecturer. Class was enjoyable with a lot of homework assignments and online quizzes.
Ryan is one of the most caring and excellent lecturer at UW. Explains things very clearly and nicely. Cares a lot about students and their success in the course.
He is a good course material deliver
Would Take Again: Yes

Definitely the best professor at UW. Super passionate about the subject he teaches, and is VERY accommodating to all his students. Homework assignment weekly for this course specifically, and TA's usually give really constructive feedback. If you have any questions, he's always ready to answer. Definitely in favor of students success :D

BEST PROFESSOR AT UW! Took him my first quarter and have no regrets. Class will be fast-paced, but you will learn so much about databases and will prepare you for the real world. I wish to be able to take another class with Professor Maas in the future.

Great lecturer- he teaches at a good pace and makes the hard concepts easy to understand. Our midterm and final were both take home. The assignments got a little harder towards the end, but were manageable and he's very approachable for questions and flexible with deadlines and extra points. Would 100% take another course with him.

The grade comprises in-class activities and four individual projects, even though he takes attendance, there're still other ways to earn extra credits to make it up. Projects are manageable if you pay attention, but you can still receive help during OH. Ryan is really caring/easy grader, just remember to be respectful by paying attention in class.

Ryan is an amazing professor thats passionate about what he teaches. You learn a lot. You aren't graded by exams but rather 4 homework that are personal projects with lots of freedom. In-class activities are graded each lecture so that you actually come to class. Best teacher and class structure at UW! Can't praise him enough. Take the class :) !

Ryan is a very good professor and very nice, but some of the teaching assistants in this class are not so helpful, they grade projects late and usually forget to post grades, sometimes you have to talk to them directly.

CSE 444 with Prof Maas was challenging in terms of projects but they were in pairs so it wasn't too bad. Prof. Maas is an amazing lecturer, like one of the best, take his time to go over database concepts and gives step-by-step animated examples. Super useful when trying to do the written homeworks! Extremely flexible with deadlines

I took CSE 344 (Intro to DBMS) and CSE 444 (Advanced DBMS) as required courses for the data science option of the computer science degree. He is the most caring, flexible for deadlines, and knowledgeable professor I have ever taken a class with at UW. Taking these classes with him is highly recommended.

Really good professor with clear and thorough lectures, really knows what he's talking about. Good communication with students and would adjust due dates and workloads if needed. Flexible and understanding of students.

All I can say is that ryan maas is the goat. There is literally nothing whatsoever to complain about him teaching this class. Ryan is probably one of the best cs professors. He is both an amazing person and professor.

Professor Maas was so so caring and kind throughout the quarter! His lectures go at a very reasonable pace and assignment guidelines/criteria are also super clear! Literally nothing I can complain about for his class.
This man is literally the greatest professor of all time. No one even comes close. If you're on the fence about taking a class with him, don't be. Undisputed, Undeniable, Understanding, G.O.A.T.
Easy class. Professor gave a great lecture. Take home midterm and final. (AU21)

One of the most caring professors I've ever met. He cares about his students a lot and is willing to accommodate for a lot of things. He is clearly passionate about teaching and his lectures are also very clear to understand. 5/5 professor.

This class was SUPER easy with Prof. Maas. I have a background in data science, so that may be why. But TAs provide a lot of help, and Prof. Maas is always available after class. Homework was some in class participation with 4 individual projects and a group project. You can complete these whenever, but if you get them done early you can get EC

He dropped the last homework because he thought the second-last homework (6th one) was too hard to us. That's very nice! One midterm and one final during the quarter and midterm could be revised to get a 100%. More than half of students in this class could get a 4.0.
I've taken several courses with him. He's such a nice guy. If you mess up something, you can talk with him and he'll definitely help you out. One of the most caring teachers at UW.
Great lectures. Really pay attention to questions and queries from students. Homework is challenging but fun.
Great professor. Lectures were well paced and course was well structured. Homework was reasonable but still well paced and challenged me well. Exams were also very stress-free and reasonable.

Pro Maas is one of the best CS professors I had so far. His pace in lectures is great not too fast and he always leaves time for questions. He is very caring of students and always tries to adjust the workload based on the situation or difficulty. Lectures are interesting. Would definitely take more classes with him.

Ryan designs the class where most people are able to get a 4.0 as long as you do all your assignments. There's 5 mini projects that you have to do using what we learn in lecture and you are able to resubmit them as many times as you would like. Participation points by answering questions about the lecture, but there is extra credit if you miss some

Lectures will feel incredibly dull at first mostly due to how he talks (very slow, quiet, and monotone), but you'll get used to it. Rewatching the lecture recordings at 1.5x definitely helps. He's very articulate and good at answering every question in depth, which is a huge plus. Expect a lot of workload without much guidance (beware of lab 3).
Great and accomodating teacher. I only have good things to say about him, tests, homework, lectures, were all very fair. Good dude

I really enjoyed having Ryan and he was flexible and conscious of workload for students. They adjusted due dates a couple of times based on what they were seeing in office hours to make sure students had plenty of time to complete the work. We had a few quizzes, but they were straight forward if you came to class and sections.
Great professor! clarify questions clearly and ask our questions in the discussion forum frequently. Nice choice. Giving points is fair
